meaning: n.辱骂,嘲弄vt.嘲弄,奚落adj.[海](桅杆)很高的
examples: He became a taunt to his neighbours.
They do not taunt you to finish what you started.
Don't chant things like "loser" or taunt them when they lose.
Pierce loves to hit big shots in New York and taunt the crowd after.
She chose to consider this not as a taunt but as a serious question.
